Question: Plane Area by integration(vertical strip)
Choice2: A = area under the curve; y = function of x; a, b = limits of integration

Question: Plane Area by integration(horizontal strip)
Choice2: A = area under the curve; x = function of y; a, b = limits of integration

Question: Plane Area by integration(2 curves)
Choice2: A = area between the two curves; y_{upper}, y_{lower} = functions of x; a, b = limits of integration

Question: Plane Area by integration(Polar coordinates)
Choice2: A = area under the curve; r = function of \theta; a, b = limits of integration

Question: Length of arc by integration
Choice2: L = length of the arc; y = function of x; a, b = limits of integration

Question: Length of arc by integration(Polar coordinates)
Choice2: L = length of the arc; r = function of \theta; a, b = limits of integration

Question: Volume of solid of revolution(circular disk)
Choice2: V = volume of the solid; R = function of x representing the radius of the disk; a, b = limits of integration

Question: Volume of solid of revolution(cylindrical shell)
Choice2: V = volume of the solid; r = function of x representing the radius of the shell; h = function of x representing the height of the shell; a, b = limits of integration

Question: Volume of solid of revolution(circular ring)
Choice2: V = volume of the solid; R = function of x representing the outer radius; r = function of x representing the inner radius; a, b = limits of integration

Question: Work
Choice2: W = work done; F(x) = force as a function of position; a, b = limits of integration representing the initial and final positions

Question: Centroid($$\bar{x}$$)
Choice2: \bar{x} = x-coordinate of the centroid; dA = area under the curve; f(x) = function representing the curve; a b = limits of integration

Question: Centroid($$\bar{y}$$)
Choice2: \bar{y} = y-coordinate of the centroid; dA = area under the curve; f(x) = function representing the curve; a b = limits of integration

Question: Moments of inertia
Choice1: I_x = \int_a^b y^2 dA; I_y = \int_a^b x^2 dA
Choice2: I_x = moment of inertia about the x-axis; I_y = moment of inertia about the y-axis; dA = area under the curve; f(x) = function representing the curve; a b = limits of integration

Question: Population Growth
Choice2: P(t) = population at time t; P_0 = initial population; r = growth rate; t = time

Question: Newton's Law of Cooling
Choice2: T(t) = temperature at time t; T_{env} = ambient temperature; T_0 = initial temperature; k = cooling constant; t = time
